growth_records.strain: Enrichment from high-rate activated sludge, Eawag WWTP Dubendorf (inoculum); reactor COD:P 200
growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 200 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate on COD basis)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.4 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 200 ... active biomass yields ... 0.36 +/- 0.07 gpCOD PHA-free gCOD removed-1 ... PHA yield ... 0.02 +/- 0.01 ... PHA content ... 0.06 +/- 0.01 ... biomass iP 13.8 +/- 1.1 mgP gpCOD-1 ... Full and stable COD-removal (>=95%)
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture (not a single prokaryote) - flagged. Steady-state chemostat, SRT=HRT=1 d => D=1/24 h=0.0417/h; at steady state mu=D=0.0417/h, doubling time=ln2/0.0417=16.6 h. Xanthobacter sp. most abundant PHA-storer (44%+/-34%) at this COD:P; 54% total PHA-storers. Yields community-level per gCOD removed. Volumetric COD uptake = D*ΔCOD ~ (1/d)*(0.95*8.4) ~= 8 gCOD/L/d (not biomass-specific; biomass conc not reported as gCOD/L in text). No secreted products (PHA is intracellular storage).

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 400 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.1 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 400 ... active biomass yield 0.35 +/- 0.04 ... PHA yield 0.07 +/- 0.04 ... PHA content 0.16 +/- 0.09 ... biomass iP 7.9 +/- 1.6
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture - flagged. D=1/24 h=0.0417/h; mu=D; doubling 16.6 h. 62% PHA-storers (17% putative). COD removal >=95%. Yields community-level per gCOD removed.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 600 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.5 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 600 ... active biomass yield 0.31 +/- 0.04 ... PHA yield 0.07 +/- 0.01 ... PHA content 0.18 +/- 0.04 ... biomass iP 5.4 +/- 0.5 ... 81% PHA-storers
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture - flagged. D=mu=0.0417/h; doubling 16.6 h. 81% PHA-storers, stable (CV 4%). COD removal >=95%. Yields community-level.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 800 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.0 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: stable microbial community consisting of >90% PHA-storers dominated by Pannonibacter sp. ... active biomass yields lowest ... 0.30 +/- 0.03 ... PHA yield highest 0.18 +/- 0.03 ... PHA content 0.36 +/- 0.05 ... biomass iP 4.0 +/- 0.3
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture (>90% PHA-storers, Pannonibacter phragmitetus dominant 78%) - flagged. Reported SRT=HRT ~1.1 d; using nominal 1 d, D=mu=0.0417/h, doubling 16.6 h. Optimal selection condition. Lowest active-biomass yield (P-limited growth) with highest PHA storage. Yields community-level per gCOD removed. COD removal >=95%.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 1000 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (60-90% of 8.0 gCOD/L at D=1/d; unstable)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 1000 ... COD-removal ... unstable ... fluctuated between 60 and 90% ... active biomass yield 0.34 +/- 0.07 ... PHA yield 0.13 +/- 0.03 ... PHA content 0.27 +/- 0.09 ... biomass iP 3.9 +/- 0.9
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture - flagged. D=mu=0.0417/h; doubling 16.6 h. P-only-limited: selection deteriorated, 57% PHA-storers, Achromobacter 18%. COD removal unstable 60-90% (uptake rate ~0.75*8=6 gCOD/L/d approximate). Yields community-level.

growth_records.strain: Laboratory-scale SBR enrichment inoculated from 'Roma Nord' municipal WWTP activated sludge (Rome, Italy); undefined consortium
growth_records.medium: Mineral medium with acetic acid (respiration pulses); SBR feast substrate = 85:15 (COD basis) acetic:propionic acid

up.compound: acetic acid (maximum, upshift-saturated qsm = qsr + qsu)
up.method: respirometry / DO mass balance; qsm=qsr+qsu at saturating substrate (Eq.9)
respirometry, Zone-1 reference endogenous rate (Table 1, n=24)

yields.Yos_O2_per_substrate: 0.26 ± 0.02 gO2/gCOD (n=24) [oxygen consumed per substrate COD; consistent with theoretical 0.26 gO2/gCOD for PHA storage without growth]

growth_records.cite: Werker, Alan et al. Modelling Mixed Microbial Culture Polyhydroxyalkanoate Accumulation Bioprocess towards Novel Methods for Polymer Production Using Dilute Volatile Fatty Acid Rich Feedstocks. Bioengineering (Basel) (2022). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering9030125. [PMC8945694]
growth_records.snippet: X a gVSS/L 1.0 ± 0.1 (11) ... q oe mgO2/gVSS/min 0.64 ± 0.12 (24) ... Y os gO2/gCOD 0.26 ± 0.02 (24) ... k s mgCOD/L 2.0 ± 0.7 (24) k u mgCOD/L 38 ± 7 (24) q s r mgCOD/gVSS/min 7.4 ± 0.1 (24) q s u mgCOD/gVSS/min 21.1 ± 1.1 (24)
growth_records.notes: Undefined mixed microbial (prokaryotic) enrichment, not a single strain. Rates measured experimentally by aerobic respirometry (dissolved-O2 mass balance) after acetic-acid pulse additions to famine biomass; substrate for the kinetic experiments was acetic acid (Fig.5). Table 1 (active-aeration experiments): Xa=1.0±0.1 gVSS/L, ka=1.11±0.14/min, qoe=0.64±0.12 mgO2/gVSS/min, Yos=0.26±0.02 gO2/gCOD, ks=2.0±0.7 mgCOD/L, ku=38±7 mgCOD/L, qsr=7.4±0.1, qsu=21.1±1.1 mgCOD/gVSS/min. Eq.9: qsm=qsr+qsu*Csi/(ku+Csi); saturating max qsm=qsr+qsu=7.4+21.1=28.5 mgCOD/gVSS/min. Unit conversion to mmol acetate/gVSS/h (acetate COD=64 gO2/mol=64 mgCOD/mmol; CH3COOH+2O2->2CO2+2H2O): qsm 28.5 mgCOD/gVSS/min *60/64 = 26.7 mmol acetate/gVSS/h; qsr 7.4*60/64=6.9 mmol/gVSS/h; qsu 21.1*60/64=19.8 mmol/gVSS/h; qoe 0.64 mgO2/gVSS/min *60/32=1.20 mmol O2/gVSS/h (gVSS used as gDW proxy). No microbial growth: authors state polymer storage without active biomass growth (measured Yos~0.26 matches theoretical storage yield), so mu/doubling-time not applicable and no growth_rate reported. Product is intracellular PHA (stored, not secreted); CO2 not measured, hence secretion_rates empty. pH 9.2 is the reported respiration-vessel pH at t=0 before acid pulse (high due to CO2 stripping under aeration). Low-substrate no-aeration runs additionally gave Yos=0.23±0.03 gO2/gCOD (n=7) and ks=0.110±0.004 mgCOD/L at Csi=5,10,15 mgCOD/L. Enrichment SBR cycle: feast feed 10 min/0.42 L, feast reaction 150 min, biomass withdrawal, famine feed, famine 552 min; OLR 8.5 gCOD/L/d; N loading 249 mgN/d; C/N 34 gCOD/gN.

growth_records.strain: Pilot-scale SBR enrichment (120 L) inoculated from Treviso full-scale WWTP activated sludge (NE Italy); undefined consortium
growth_records.medium: Fermentate of 33:67 (v/v) OFMSW + waste activated sludge (VFA-rich; 75% soluble COD as VFA, butyric+acetic dominated); acetic acid used for respiration pulses

up.compound: acetic acid (maximum, upshift-saturated qsm = qsr + qsu)
up.method: respirometry / DO mass balance; qsm=qsr+qsu at saturating substrate (Eq.9)
respirometry, Zone-1 reference endogenous rate (Table 1, n=17)

growth_records.cite: Werker, Alan et al. Modelling Mixed Microbial Culture Polyhydroxyalkanoate Accumulation Bioprocess towards Novel Methods for Polymer Production Using Dilute Volatile Fatty Acid Rich Feedstocks. Bioengineering (Basel) (2022). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering9030125. [PMC8945694]
growth_records.notes: Undefined mixed microbial (prokaryotic) enrichment, not a single strain. Experimental aerobic respirometry (DO mass balance) after acetic-acid pulse additions to pilot famine biomass. Table 1 (active aeration, n=17): Xa=1.71±0.04 gVSS/L, ka=0.60±0.15/min, qoe=0.25±0.07 mgO2/gVSS/min, Yos=0.23±0.05 gO2/gCOD, ks=1.8±0.4 mgCOD/L, ku=20±2 mgCOD/L, qsr=2.5±0.2, qsu=6.8±0.2 mgCOD/gVSS/min. Eq.9 saturating max qsm=qsr+qsu=2.5+6.8=9.3 mgCOD/gVSS/min. Conversion (acetate 64 mgCOD/mmol): qsm 9.3*60/64=8.7 mmol acetate/gVSS/h; qsr 2.5*60/64=2.34; qsu 6.8*60/64=6.38 mmol/gVSS/h; qoe 0.25*60/32=0.47 mmol O2/gVSS/h. No growth (PHA storage-only, Yos~theoretical), so no mu/doubling time. Product PHA is intracellular (not secreted); CO2 not measured -> secretion_rates empty. Pilot SBR fed VFA-rich fermentate from 33:67 v/v OFMSW+WAS (Treviso); medium chemically undefined (complex fermentate) hence medium_composition_stated=false.
